seo_description: Current time in Warsaw, Poland — live Central European Time (CET/CEST) clock, DST schedule, and time differences with London, New York, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Warsaw right now?
faq.answer: Warsaw, Poland uses Central European Time (CET, UTC+1) in winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST, UTC+2) during Daylight Saving Time in summer. Clocks spring forward on the last Sunday in March and fall back on the last Sunday in October, following EU DST rules. The IANA timezone identifier is Europe/Warsaw.

faq.question: What time zone is Warsaw in?
faq.answer: Warsaw is in the Central European Time zone (CET, UTC+1 in winter; CEST, UTC+2 in summer). Poland follows EU DST rules, switching clocks on the same dates as Germany, France, Italy, and other Central European countries. Warsaw shares its timezone with Berlin, Prague, Vienna, and Budapest. The IANA identifier is Europe/Warsaw.

faq.question: Does Warsaw observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Poland observes EU Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour to CEST (UTC+2) on the last Sunday in March at 2:00 AM and fall back to CET (UTC+1) on the last Sunday in October at 3:00 AM. Poland follows the EU DST schedule consistently.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Warsaw and London?
faq.answer: Warsaw (CET, UTC+1) is 1 hour ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During European summer (CEST, UTC+2) and UK summer (BST, UTC+1), Warsaw is still 1 hour ahead of London. The 1-hour gap is maintained year-round because both switch to summer time on the same date.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Warsaw and New York?
faq.answer: Warsaw (CET, UTC+1) is 6 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Warsaw is 5 hours ahead. When Warsaw moves to CEST (UTC+2) and New York is on EDT (UTC-4), Warsaw is 6 hours ahead. The gap varies during brief spring/autumn windows when only one side has transitioned.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Warsaw and Berlin?
faq.answer: Warsaw (Europe/Warsaw, CET/CEST) and Berlin (Europe/Berlin, CET/CEST) are on the same timezone year-round. Both switch to summer time on the same date. There is no time difference between Warsaw and Berlin.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Warsaw?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Warsaw are 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM or 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CET/CEST, Monday through Friday. Warsaw is Poland's capital and largest city, a major financial and technology hub in Central and Eastern Europe. The Warsaw Stock Exchange (GPW) is the largest exchange in Central Europe, trading 9:00 AM–5:00 PM CET/CEST. Poland is a major BPO/IT outsourcing destination for Western European firms.
